campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how much money the city has invested and where it's being spent. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, like potentially adding bike lanes, better bus routes, or other ways to get around town without a car.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and spending report
City treasurer's monthly financial update
New multimodal transportation improvements (biking, public transit, walking)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or law. After hearing from residents, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city rule) and decided to skip the usual first reading step to move things along faster. The ordinance will be voted on for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Ordinance placed on consent calendar (streamlined approval process)

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several previous sessions in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focused on documenting past decisions and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and plan upcoming council sessions. They reviewed what items would appear on the April 21st and April 28th meeting agendas, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked on any special notice requirements for documents being discussed.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
Scheduled specific time slots for agenda items as needed
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the budget and spending plans for city projects in 2026-27, then officially recognized both Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget planning for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month proclamation to recognize local youth
National Volunteer Week proclamation to honor community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting minutes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in the city. The main focus was introducing new amendments to the city's residential tenant protections program that could affect renters and landlords in Sunnyvale.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New amendments to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code
Changes to the Residential Tenant Protections Program
Public hearing on housing and tenant issues

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026, to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that lets you see how the city spends your tax dollars. The council also reminded people that if you're trying to influence city decisions through lobbying, you need to register and report your activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and community training
City budget overview and spending breakdown
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
How to report lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026, to discuss changes to the city's charter (its basic governing rules). Chair Pat Nikolai joined the meeting remotely from Oregon, while the rest of the council met in person and allowed residents to join either way.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings - discussing potential updates to city governing rules
Remote participation options available via Zoom for residents who can't attend in person
Public comment submission deadline of 10 AM for written statements to the city attorney
In-person and remote meeting access for community members

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan (2025-2030) and considering changes to how the council fills vacant seats. The meeting also covered routine items like approving previous meeting notes and scheduling the next meeting.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Changes to the process for filling empty council seats
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a more important historic resource, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the building to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building's historic status being upgraded from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area allowed for seismic and historic rehabilitation work
Downtown Commercial zone project in pedestrian shopping district
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments approved
